items.title: I think the Knicks, Thunder and Spurs will have plenty to say about that. And everyone is still putting their teams together.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
I think the Knicks, Thunder and Spurs will have plenty to say about that. And everyone is still putting their teams together.

Also, building a roster is about getting talent that fits, not just collecting talent. Philly should be very, very good, but favorites is too far for me.

items.title: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Anfernee Simons had interest from the Philadelphia 76ers, Golden State Warriors, Miami Heat, Denver Nuggets, Dallas Mavericks, and Indiana Pacers, league sources said. Simons ultimately agreed to a two-year, $12.3 million deal, including a player option, as ESPN first reported.

items.title: Love the potential "one free throw" rule change. Not sure what the data suggests, but my assumption is that players miss the first FT slightly more than others (for stamina and repitition reasons) and that therefore it would slightly decrease scoring overall.
items.author: Mason Ginsberg
items.body_excerpt: Love the potential "one free throw" rule change. Not sure what the data suggests, but my assumption is that players miss the first FT slightly more than others (for stamina and repitition reasons) and that therefore it would slightly decrease scoring overall.

items.title: A strong move from the Sixers to bolster their bench and add much-needed three-point shooting with Anfernee Simons. His 2025-26 season shows he can thrive in the role he is set to inherit in Philadelphia.
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
A strong move from the Sixers to bolster their bench and add much-needed three-point shooting with Anfernee Simons. His 2025-26 season shows he can thrive in the role he is set to inherit in Philadelphia.

The Sixers have fired their free-agency bullets:

www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

items.title: The Simons signing is ~ the bi-annual exception. Sixers are in the tax. Only $4 or $5m below apron 1. One roster spot left.
items.author: Austin Krell
items.body_excerpt:
The Simons signing is ~ the bi-annual exception. Sixers are in the tax. Only $4 or $5m below apron 1. One roster spot left. 

Career 38% 3p shooter on 6.6 attempts per game. Just turned 27.

items.title: This will use up the remainder of the Sixers' MLE, pushing the Hukporti signing into the bi-annual exception.
items.author: Derek Bodner
items.body_excerpt:
This will use up the remainder of the Sixers' MLE, pushing the Hukporti signing into the bi-annual exception.

The Sixers now have $2.062m remaining on the bi-annual, and a $4.2 million trade exception, at their disposal if they wish.

They are $4.3 million below the hard cap.

items.title: Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Love that Anfernee Simons contract for the Sixers. What a killer get to give them a third guard.

Looks like he'll get the rest of the NTMLE (splitting it with Dean Wade), which means that Ariel Hukporti's contract will probably come out of the Bi-Annual Exception.

items.title: Can confirm via a league source that the Sixers have agreed to sign Anfernee Simons to a two-year, $12.3 million contract with a player option for the second year. There's your bench guard with scoring punch and shooting.
